# Task Log

<!-- Automatically maintained by Claude Code. Do not edit task numbers. -->
<!-- Use /audit purge N to remove items 1 through N after confirming they're good. -->

- [Pending QA] 1. **Restructure repo for Google Docs + online Word documents** — Moved Apps Script files (Claude.gs, Code.gs, Triggers.gs, appsscript.json) into `Google Docs/` via `git mv` (history preserved). Created `Word Online/` with a full Office Add-in: `manifest.xml`, `taskpane.html`, `taskpane.css`, `Code.js` (mirrors Code.gs + Triggers.gs), and `Claude.js` (mirrors Claude.gs, same system prompt and JSON contract, uses `anthropic-dangerous-direct-browser-access: true`). Rewrote `README.md` to document both integrations with separate setup sections and a unified troubleshooting block. The Word version reads comments via `document.getComments()`, applies edits via `body.search().insertText('Replace')`, adds anchored comments via `range.insertComment()`, and replies via `comment.reply()` — all WordApi 1.4+. Auto-polling uses `setInterval(5 min)` while the task pane is open (Office Add-ins can't run while the doc is closed — this is called out in the README).
- [Pending QA] 2. **Commit to git and push to origin** — Pushed commit 2ba03c1 to origin/main. `a89e98c..2ba03c1  main -> main`.
- [Pending QA] 3. **No Upload option in Word Online Add-ins** — Dan got it installed via alternative method (resolved).
- [Pending QA] 4. **Fix "context.document.getComments is not a function"** — Fixed both call sites in Code.js to use `context.document.body.getComments()`. Commit d5be0ec pushed to origin. Dan still hitting the error at 10:00 AM — cached JS. Add cache-busting (task #6).
- [Pending QA] 5. **Persistent API key UX** — Split into saved view (masked key + Change / Remove buttons) and edit view (input + Save / Cancel). Key is only re-entered when Dan wants to change it. Commit 62c2b95.
- [Pending QA] 6. **Cache-bust task pane assets** — Added no-cache meta tags and `?v=3` query strings on JS/CSS. Commit 62c2b95.
- [Pending QA] 7. **"0 edits applied / string not found" when adding a bullet to a list**
- [Pending QA] 8. **Add an icon for the app in the ribbon** — Generated 16/32/80 PNGs via `generate_icons.py`. Verified live at GitHub Pages (HTTP 200, valid PNG). Version-bump re-upload (1.0.1.0) didn't clear the broken icon, so the root cause was likely the space in the `Word Online/` folder name: even `%20`-encoded URLs can misbehave in Office's icon pipeline. Renamed folders to `word-online/` and `google-docs/`, updated manifest URLs and README, bumped manifest to 1.0.2.0.
- [Pending QA] 9. **Bullet formatting lost when inserting a new list item** — Root cause: the paragraph-level annotation logic that was supposed to tag bullets as `[Bullet L0]` had been lost in an earlier rewrite. The prompt was confidently telling Claude to anchor on `[Bullet L*]` lines but none were being emitted. Restored `readDocState` + `annotateParagraph` in commit 4fbf279; Dan confirmed bullets work after the defensive commit 76d5500 which handled cache-stale `docAnnotated: undefined` gracefully.
- [In Progress] 11. **Inserted content inherits heading style** — Dan asked Claude to add content to the "Notes" heading section; the new paragraph came in as Heading 2 because `Paragraph.insertParagraph('After')` copies the anchor's style by default. Fix: after insertion (non-list branch only), choose a style via `defaultStyleFor(anchorStyleBuiltIn)` — `Normal` if the anchor is a heading/title/subtitle, otherwise fall through. Extended the `inserts` contract with an optional `style` field so Claude can override (e.g., adding a new `"Heading 2"` subsection explicitly). Bumped to ?v=14.
- [Pending QA] 10. **General formatting awareness** — Dan wants to make sure Claude in general understands document formatting (headings, bullets, quotes, etc.), not just plain text. Implementation: read each paragraph's `styleBuiltIn` and `listItemOrNullObject/level` in `readDocState`; build an annotated doc view where each paragraph is prefixed with `[Heading 1]`, `[Bullet L0]`, `[Title]`, `[Subtitle]`, `[Quote]`, etc.; pass that annotated view to Claude instead of the plain body.text; update the system prompt to explain the annotation format and instruct Claude to strip style tags when writing back `original_text` / `after_text` / `quoted_text`. This gives Claude structural context for every edit it makes. — Screenshot shows Claude tried to add `john@home.com` to an Attendees bullet list and update the count `(3)` → `(4)`, but 0 edits applied and 1 string not found. Root cause: `body.search()` can't match text across paragraph breaks, and `insertText` can't create new paragraphs anyway. Fix: add a new `inserts` action type in Claude's JSON contract (`after_text` + `new_paragraphs`) and apply it via `paragraph.insertParagraph('After')` which inherits list formatting. Also: skip multi-line edits with a clear log message, and surface the actual missed strings in the activity log so the failure mode is diagnosable.
